title: rhamnus × gayeri
intro: Rhamnus × gayeri, also known as Gayer's buckthorn, is a hybrid plant species in the family Rhamnaceae, derived from the cross between Rhamnus cathartica and Rhamnus pallasii. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include being a rich source of anthraquinones, a type of compound that has laxative and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Rhamnus × gayeri include its ability to stimulate digestion, relieve constipation, and reduce inflammation, making it a valuable treatment for various gastrointestinal issues. In herbal preparations, Rhamnus × gayeri is often used as a natural laxative or combined with other herbs to create a digestive tonic, and it is also used in the production of various pharmaceuticals due to its unique anthraquinone content.
